trivia_bullets.text: 'Anthropede' is a portmanteau of 'anthropo-' (Greek for human) and 'centipede,' describing a centipede whose legs are human arms. Consistent with Duskmourn's body-horror design philosophy fusing mundane human form with monstrous anatomy to maximize unease.
sources: Wizards of the Coast: Duskmourn vision design handoffs, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: "The touch of its many hands is almost gentle, at first. But then the grip tightens, and the mandibles dig in." — no named speaker; mirrors the creature's visual of deceptive softness turning to lethal force.

ETB can destroy a target Room (Duskmourn's new dual-door enchantment split card subtype representing haunted-house areas). Discard-or-pay-{2} cost makes it a flexible answer to opponent Room strategies; on-flavor as a creature that literally dismantles the House's architecture.
sources: Wizards of the Coast: Duskmourn mechanics article — Room design

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 24,723 as of 2026-05-19 — lower-adoption DSK common in commander formats. Room-hate is context-dependent; outside Room-heavy metagames, a 3/4 Reach for {3}{G} is unremarkable.

Artist Loïc Canavaggia is a French freelance illustrator based in Cuq-Toulza, France, with 45+ MTG card credits including Universe Beyond Fallout, Murders at Karlov Manor, Modern Horizons III, and Lost Caverns of Ixalan (where he served as digital artist lead). Also works on Paizo/Pathfinder projects.

related_cards.note: Fellow DSK body-horror creature; same set, shared decay-and-infestation aesthetic

related_cards.name: Arabella, Abandoned Doll
related_cards.note: Room-synergy commander; Rooms are what Anthropede destroys
